---
title: "Phase-type distribution graphs for population genetics"
data: today
author: "Kasper Munch"
institute: BiRC, Department of Molecular Biology and Genetics, Aarhus University
format:
  revealjs: 
    logo: images/au_segl.svg
    # footer: "Kasper Munch"  
    controls-layout: bottom-right    
    slide-number: c
    classoption: fleqn
    fleqn: true
    # margin: 0.03
    mainfont: "Helvetica"
    # width: "100%"
    # height: "100%"
    # min-scale: 1
    # max-scale: 1    
    # # smaller: true
    # # width: 1050	# Normal width (defaults to 1050)
    # # height:	700 # Normal height (defaults to 700)
    # # width: 933	# "standard"
    # # height:	700 # "standard"
    # # width: 1050 # "wide"
    # # height: 590 # "wide"
    # width: 1244 # "wide"
    # height:	700 # "wide"
---

```{=html}
<!--
# ARROWS: 
↑ ↓ ← → ↔ ↕
← → ↔ ↑ ↓ • ↕ • ↖ ↗ ↘ ↙ • ⤡ ⤢
⬅ ( ⮕ ➡ ) ⬆ ⬇ • ⬌ ⬍ • ⬉ ⬈ ⬊ ⬋
⇦ ⇨ ⇧ ⇩ • ⬁ ⬀ ⬂ ⬃ • ⬄ ⇳
➛ ➜ ➔ ➝ ➞ ➟ ➠ ➧ ➨
⭠ ⭢ ⭤ ⭡ ⭣ ⭥ 
-->
```

<!-- =============================================================================== -->
<!-- =============================================================================== -->
# Week 1
<!-- =============================================================================== -->
<!-- =============================================================================== -->


<!-- Red text: -->
<!-- [important note]{.alert} -->


<!-- =============================================================================== -->
<!-- =============================================================================== -->
# Week 2
<!-- =============================================================================== -->
<!-- =============================================================================== -->

## Slide


<!-- =============================================================================== -->
<!-- =============================================================================== -->
# Other Research...
<!-- =============================================================================== -->
<!-- =============================================================================== -->

## Three connected topics

::: {.absolute top="15%" left="10%" width="50%"}
![](images/x-y-auto-armsrace.png)
:::
::: {.absolute top="10%" left="60%" width="30%"}
![](images/x-y-incompat.png)
:::
::: {.absolute top="60%" left="30%" width="40%"}
![](images/drive-and-autism.png)
:::

## Baboons 

<!-- ::: {.absolute top="10%" left="10%" width="40%"}
![](images/cell-genomics-graphical-abstract.png)
::: -->
<!-- ::: {.absolute top="10%" left="30%" width="40%"}
![](images/science-online-figure.png)
::: -->
::: {.absolute top="10%" left="15%" width="80%"}
![](images/fig1-eriks-paper.png)
:::

## chrX and genomic "swampings"

::: {.absolute top="10%" left="15%" width="80%"}
![](images/baboon-lai-chrX-chr8.png)
:::

## Spermatogenesis scRNA

::: {.absolute top="25%" left="10%" width="40%"}
![](images/scRNA-spermatogenesis.png)
Mikkel Schierup
:::
::: {.absolute top="20%" left="50%" width="40%"}
![](images/CFAP47-trajectory.png)
Meritxell Riera Belles
:::

## Sperm chromatin compartments

::: {.absolute top="10%" left="10%" width="80%"}
![](images/sperm-a-b-compartments.png)
:::
::: {.absolute top="45%" left="15%" width="70%"}
![](images/sperm-A-B-cDEG-ideogram.png)
:::

## Large Danish Autism cohorts


![](images/ipsych.png){.r-stretch}

## Three way overlap

::: {.absolute top="10%" left="30%" width="40%"}
![](images/three-way-venn.png)
(CDKL5, CLCN4, HUWE1, IL1RAPL1, PTCHD1)
:::



<!-- =============================================================================== -->
<!-- =============================================================================== -->
# Menu
<!-- =============================================================================== -->
<!-- =============================================================================== -->


<!-- =============================================================================== -->
## Why?

- Motivations:
  - Exploration of statistical properties.
  - Can we do with less data if you model more of its properties? 
  - What can be computed given enough time and money? And can we share such precomputed models?
  - How far can we take inference on unphased data?
  - Bayesian inference with true posterior.

<!-- =============================================================================== -->
## What?

::::: {layout="[0.55,-0.05,0.4]"}

::: column

- Represents phase-type distributions as graphs.

::: spacer-1
:::

- **Exact**:
  - Takes phase-type distributions from theory to practice.
  - Continuous and discrete
  - Moments and PMFs
  - Sojourn times.
  - Joint probability distribution over discrete events

::: spacer-1
:::

- **Arb. approx.**:
  - State probabilities at time t.
  - PDF

:::
::: column

- **Time inhomogeneous models**:
  - Joint probabilities at time t.
  - Moment matched distributions
  - (full PDF)

::: spacer-1
:::

- **Inference**:
  - SVGD
  - Method of moments
  - MCMC

:::
:::::

<!-- =============================================================================== -->
## Code

```{.python code-line-numbers="1-3|4,6|7"}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

r = np.arange(0, 2, 0.01)
theta = 2 * np.pi * r
fig, ax = plt.subplots(subplot_kw={'projection': 'polar'})
ax.plot(theta, r)
ax.set_rticks([0.5, 1, 1.5, 2])
ax.grid(True)
plt.show()
```

<!-- =============================================================================== -->
## Docs

::: {.absolute top="10%" left=0 width="100%"}
![](images/phasic-docs.png)

:::

<!-- =============================================================================== -->
## The coalescent is a **Markov jump process**

:::: {.absolute top="25%"}
::: r-stack

![](./images/coal_mc_jump_anim_2-nostart.svg){.nostretch .fragment width="100%" fig-align="center"}

![](./images/coal_mc_jump_anim_3-nostart.svg){.nostretch .fragment width="100%" fig-align="center"}

![](./images/coal_mc_jump_anim_4-nostart.svg){.nostretch .fragment width="100%" fig-align="center"}

![](./images/coal_mc_jump_anim_5-nostart.svg){.nostretch .fragment width="100%" fig-align="center"}
:::
::::


<!-- =============================================================================== -->
## Phase-type distribution with **rewards**

::::::: {layout="[[-0.1,0.45,-0.05,0.4], [-0.1,0.45,-0.05,0.4]]"}
::: column
![](./images/coalescent-unit-rewards.svg)
:::

::: {.column }
**TMRCA (tree height):**

${\color{red} \boldsymbol{r}} = (1,\ 1,\ 1,\ 1)$

$\mathbb{E}[\tau] = \boldsymbol{\alpha} \boldsymbol{U}\!\, \triangle \! \,({\color{red}\boldsymbol{r}})\, \mathbf{e}$
:::

::: {.column }
![](./images/coalescent-tot-brlen-rewards.svg)
:::

::: {.column }
**Total branch length:**

${\color{red} \boldsymbol{r}} = (4,\ 3,\ 2,\ 2)$

$\mathbb{E}[\tau] = \boldsymbol{\alpha} \boldsymbol{U}\!\, \triangle \! \,({\color{red}\boldsymbol{r}})\, \mathbf{e}$
:::
:::::::

<!-- =============================================================================== -->
## **Graph** formulation

::: {.absolute top="15%" left="50%" width="60%"}
![](./images/sparse-matrix-as-graph.png){.nostretch width="100%"}
:::

::: spacer-1
:::

:::: {layout="[[0.45,-0.05,0.45], [0.45,-0.05,0.45]]"}
:::: column
**Analytically tractable** 😀 

::: spacer-1
:::

- As graph algorithms:
  - Elimination
  - Exponentiation

- Allows:
  - Decomposition
  - Parallelization
  - Parametrization
  - Caching
  - Sharing
  - Inference

::::
